issues: The draft says 'five titles including the Mowat Cup, Abbott Cup, and Centennial Cup' — the OCR lists five titles as: B.C. Junior League, Mowat Cup, Alberta-B.C. (Doyle Cup), Abbott Cup, and Centennial Cup. The draft omits the B.C. Junior League and Alberta-B.C. titles and implies only three are listed.

The draft credits goals to 'Dave Tomlinson, Jason Phillips, and Paul Rutherford' but omits a fifth goal and the empty-net goal by Phillips. Per OCR: Tomlinson at 16:53 and Phillips at 18:13 in period 1; Rutherford at 8:48 of period 3; Phillips again at 13:28; and Phillips into the empty net at 19:07 — five total Sockeyes goals.
The draft says 'Phillips was named player-of-the-game' — the OCR states Romeo was player-of-the-game in all three games he appeared in (not Phillips). Phillips won most sportsmanlike player.
The byline is listed as 'Elliott Pap' in the draft but the OCR clearly shows 'By ELLIOTT PAP, Sun Sports Reporter' — the name is correct but the byline format should reflect the full credit as given.
The headline in the draft ('Sockeyes add Centennial Cup to their silverware collection') matches the OCR exactly — no issue there.

The draft does not mention the photo caption ('CHAMPAGNE SHOWER anoints Richmond Sockeyes coach Orland Kurtenbach') or the post-Cup human interest angle about Kurtenbach's uncertain return and Bruce Taylor's comments, which are central to the article.
review.correctedDescriptionShort: Richmond Sockeyes defeat the Humboldt Broncos 5-2 in Saskatchewan to claim the Centennial Cup, with goaltender Frank Romeo named tournament MVP and coach Orland Kurtenbach's future with the club left uncertain.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: The Richmond Sockeyes defeated the Humboldt Broncos 5-2 on Saturday in Humboldt, Saskatchewan to win the Centennial Cup, Canada's tier-two junior hockey championship. Goals by Dave Tomlinson and Jason Phillips in the first period set the tone, with Paul Rutherford adding a breakaway goal in the third; Phillips contributed a hat trick including the empty-netter. Goaltender Frank Romeo — player-of-the-game in all three of his tournament appearances — earned MVP honours, while Phillips took the most sportsmanlike player award. The victory capped a post-season in which Richmond won 27 of 34 playoff games and five titles (BCJHL, Mowat Cup, Alberta-BC, Abbott Cup, Centennial Cup); coach Orland Kurtenbach said he would decide over the summer whether to return to defend the title, with owner Bruce Taylor vowing not to pressure him.
review.correctedHeadline: Sockeyes add Centennial Cup to their silverware collection
review.correctedByline: By Elliott Pap, Sun Sports Reporter
review.notes: The photo caption at the top of the OCR ("CHAMPAGNE SHOWER anoints Richmond Sockeyes coach Orland Kurtenbach…") is credited to Canadian Press and is a separate image element on the page. The article text is attributed to Elliott Pap. The draft had two notable factual errors: it misidentified who won player-of-the-game (Romeo, not Phillips), and it understated both the number of goals scored and the full list of five titles won.
